title: Classifier-Free Guidance Steps
concept: Blending prompt-conditioned and unconditioned noise predictions to control prompt adherence.

exercises.prompt: What happens after we predict the noise in the DDIM step?

options.text: We add noise to the image.
We subtract some of the predicted noise to step backwards.
We keep the noise as it is.

exercises.explain: Subtracting a fraction of the predicted noise lets us move back towards a clearer image.

cards.title: Understanding Classifier-Free Guidance
cards.body: Classifier-Free Guidance helps us combine different predictions to adjust how closely our generated image follows the prompt.

cards.title: What is Classifier-Free Guidance?
cards.body: It blends predictions based on a prompt with unconditioned predictions, allowing us to steer the final result more towards what we want.

cards.title: Mixing Colors for Better Art
cards.body: Think of this like mixing paint colors: just as you can create a new color by blending two together, we blend predictions to create an image that better matches our prompt.

cards.title: Using Classifier-Free Guidance
cards.body: If we want an image of a 'red car,' we blend noise predictions from this specific idea with general ones to enhance adherence to the prompt.

cards.title: Myth: All Predictions Are Equal
cards.body: It's a common misconception that all predictions in image generation are the same; the blend we create with Classifier-Free Guidance makes a significant difference!

cards.title: Combining Predictions
cards.body: On the left, a prompt to paint a 'blue sky' is given. On the right, we show how we combine that prompt with general sky predictions for improved results.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of Classifier-Free Guidance?

options.text: It allows predictions to be random.
It blends prompt and unconditioned predictions for better adherence.
It simplifies the noise predictions entirely.
It removes any influence of the prompt.

exercises.explain: Blending predictions enhances the image generation process by ensuring the output closely aligns with the specified prompt.

exercises.prompt: Classifier-Free Guidance allows us to ignore the prompt entirely.
exercises.explain: This statement is false; Classifier-Free Guidance actively uses the prompt to guide the predictions made.

exercises.prompt: To control how closely our generated image adheres to the prompt, we use __________ guidance steps.
exercises.sentence: To control how closely our generated image adheres to the prompt, we use classifier-free guidance steps.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'classifier-free', which refers to the method of blending predictions to enhance image adherence.

exercises.prompt: Which statement correctly describes Classifier-Free Guidance?

options.text: It combines guided and unconditioned predictions to steer output closely to the intent.
It eliminates the effect of noise in images.

exercises.explain: Correct answer: It effectively combines predictions, allowing us to get the best of both guided prompts and general predictions.

exercises.prompt: Why is Classifier-Free Guidance important in the image generation process?

options.text: It makes the images completely random and unpredictable.
It enhances the alignment between the generated images and the given prompts.
It focuses only on the noise prediction for clarity.
It ignores the condition of the prompt for randomness.

exercises.explain: By enhancing the alignment with prompts, Classifier-Free Guidance allows us to create images that not only look good but also fulfill the specific creative vision associated with the prompt. Keep practicing, and you'll be ready for the next exciting lesson!
